sections.heading: TNM
sections.content: {"system": "Ann Arbor staging system with Cotswolds modification [6,7]; each stage divided into A (no systemic symptoms) and B (presence of B symptoms: unexplained fevers >38°C, drenching night sweats, or weight loss >10% body weight within 6 months)", "t_stages": [{"stage": "N/A", "description": "Hodgkin lymphoma does not use a formal TNM staging system. Staging is based on the Ann Arbor classification system, which defines stage based on extent of lymph node and extranodal involvement relative to the diaphragm."}], "n_stages": [{"stage": "Stage I", "description": "Involvement of a single lymph node region (I) or localized involvement of a single extralymphatic organ or site (IE)"}, {"stage": "Stage II", "description": "Involvement of two or more lymph node regions on the same side of the diaphragm (II) or localized involvement of a single associated extralymphatic organ or site and its regional lymph node(s), with or without involvement of other lymph node regions on the same side of the diaphragm (IIE). Number of lymph node regions may be indicated by subscript (e.g., II3)"}, {"stage": "Stage III", "description": "Involvement of lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm (III), which may also be accompanied by localized involvement of an associated extranodal organ or site (IIIE), by involvement of the spleen (IIIS), or by both (IIIE+S)"}, {"stage": "Stage IV", "description": "Disseminated (multifocal) involvement of one or more extranodal organs, with or without associated lymph node involvement, or isolated extranodal organ involvement with distant (nonregional) nodal involvement"}], "m_stages": [{"stage": "N/A", "description": "Hodgkin lymphoma uses extranodal designations (E, S, X) rather than formal M categories. E = extranodal extension; S = splenic involvement; X = bulky disease. Stage IV represents disseminated extranodal involvement."}], "stage_groups": [{"group": "Stage IA/IIA Favorable Disease (GHSG criteria)", "criteria": "Stage I-II with no unfavorable factors: MMR ≤0.33, ≤2 nodal regions, ESR <50 mm/hr (or <30 mm/hr if B symptoms), no E-lesions, no bulky disease >10 cm", "clinical_meaning": "Limited disease with favorable prognosis; amenable to abbreviated chemotherapy plus ISRT or chemotherapy alone", "five_yr_survival": "5-year PFS approximately 93-94% with 2 cycles ABVD + ISRT 20 Gy (HD16 data); 5-year OS approximately 97%", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage IA/IIA Favorable Disease (EORTC criteria)", "criteria": "Stage I-II with ≤3 nodal regions involved (including mediastinum and bilateral hila as single region; infraclavicular/subpectoral combined with axilla), no bulky mediastinal disease, no ESR ≥50 mm/hr, no B symptoms, no E-lesions", "clinical_meaning": "Limited disease favorable prognosis; broader favorable criteria than GHSG", "five_yr_survival": "3-year PFS approximately 94.6% with CMT (RAPID data); 5-year PFS approximately 98.8% with ABVD + INRT (H10F data)", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage I/II Unfavorable Disease", "criteria": "Stage I-II with any unfavorable factor: B symptoms, bulky mediastinal disease (MMR >0.33 or MTR >0.35), ESR ≥50 mm/hr (or ≥30 mm/hr if B), ≥3 nodal regions (GHSG) or ≥4 nodal regions (EORTC/NCCN), E-lesions, or adenopathy >10 cm", "clinical_meaning": "Limited disease with adverse prognostic features; requires more intensive treatment", "five_yr_survival": "2-year PFS approximately 97.3% with BV-AVD + ISRT (BREACH data); PFS outcomes improved with BrECADD for stage IIB with large mediastinal mass", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage III-IV Advanced Disease", "criteria": "Stage III: lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm; Stage IV: disseminated extranodal involvement. With or without bulky disease, B symptoms, or elevated IPS", "clinical_meaning": "Advanced-stage disease requiring systemic chemotherapy; IPS may further stratify prognosis", "five_yr_survival": "2-year PFS approximately 92% with nivolumab-AVD (SWOG S1826); 4-year PFS approximately 94.3% with BrECADD (HD21); 6-year OS approximately 93.9% with BV-AVD (ECHELON-1)", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage III-IV (Older adults/unfit)", "criteria": "Stage III-IV in patients >60 years or those unfit for intensive therapy; associated with poorer outcomes, more B symptoms, mixed cellularity histology, EBV+ disease, and medical comorbidities", "clinical_meaning": "Requires modified treatment approach with reduced toxicity regimens; limited prospective data for alternatives to standard therapies", "five_yr_survival": "2-year PFS approximately 89% with nivolumab-AVD in patients ≥60 years (S1826 subset analysis); 2-year EFS approximately 80% with sequential BV-AVD (Evens et al)", "treatment_intent": "Curative, with goal of minimizing toxicity while maximizing efficacy"}], "staging_pearls": ["The number of lymph node regions involved is indicated by subscript (e.g., II3) per Ann Arbor classification", "GHSG and EORTC define ly

t_stages.description: Hodgkin lymphoma does not use a formal TNM staging system. Staging is based on the Ann Arbor classification system, which defines stage based on extent of lymph node and extranodal involvement relative to the diaphragm.

n_stages.description: Involvement of a single lymph node region (I) or localized involvement of a single extralymphatic organ or site (IE)

Involvement of two or more lymph node regions on the same side of the diaphragm (II) or localized involvement of a single associated extralymphatic organ or site and its regional lymph node(s), with or without involvement of other lymph node regions on the same side of the diaphragm (IIE). Number of lymph node regions may be indicated by subscript (e.g., II3)

Involvement of lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm (III), which may also be accompanied by localized involvement of an associated extranodal organ or site (IIIE), by involvement of the spleen (IIIS), or by both (IIIE+S)

Disseminated (multifocal) involvement of one or more extranodal organs, with or without associated lymph node involvement, or isolated extranodal organ involvement with distant (nonregional) nodal involvement

m_stages.description: Hodgkin lymphoma uses extranodal designations (E, S, X) rather than formal M categories. E = extranodal extension; S = splenic involvement; X = bulky disease. Stage IV represents disseminated extranodal involvement.

stage_groups.criteria: Stage I-II with no unfavorable factors: MMR ≤0.33, ≤2 nodal regions, ESR <50 mm/hr (or <30 mm/hr if B symptoms), no E-lesions, no bulky disease >10 cm
stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Limited disease with favorable prognosis; amenable to abbreviated chemotherapy plus ISRT or chemotherapy alone
stage_groups.five_yr_survival: 5-year PFS approximately 93-94% with 2 cycles ABVD + ISRT 20 Gy (HD16 data); 5-year OS approximately 97%

stage_groups.criteria: Stage I-II with ≤3 nodal regions involved (including mediastinum and bilateral hila as single region; infraclavicular/subpectoral combined with axilla), no bulky mediastinal disease, no ESR ≥50 mm/hr, no B symptoms, no E-lesions
stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Limited disease favorable prognosis; broader favorable criteria than GHSG
stage_groups.five_yr_survival: 3-year PFS approximately 94.6% with CMT (RAPID data); 5-year PFS approximately 98.8% with ABVD + INRT (H10F data)

stage_groups.criteria: Stage I-II with any unfavorable factor: B symptoms, bulky mediastinal disease (MMR >0.33 or MTR >0.35), ESR ≥50 mm/hr (or ≥30 mm/hr if B), ≥3 nodal regions (GHSG) or ≥4 nodal regions (EORTC/NCCN), E-lesions, or adenopathy >10 cm
stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Limited disease with adverse prognostic features; requires more intensive treatment
stage_groups.five_yr_survival: 2-year PFS approximately 97.3% with BV-AVD + ISRT (BREACH data); PFS outcomes improved with BrECADD for stage IIB with large mediastinal mass

stage_groups.criteria: Stage III: lymph node regions on both sides of the diaphragm; Stage IV: disseminated extranodal involvement. With or without bulky disease, B symptoms, or elevated IPS
stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Advanced-stage disease requiring systemic chemotherapy; IPS may further stratify prognosis
stage_groups.five_yr_survival: 2-year PFS approximately 92% with nivolumab-AVD (SWOG S1826); 4-year PFS approximately 94.3% with BrECADD (HD21); 6-year OS approximately 93.9% with BV-AVD (ECHELON-1)

stage_groups.criteria: Stage III-IV in patients >60 years or those unfit for intensive therapy; associated with poorer outcomes, more B symptoms, mixed cellularity histology, EBV+ disease, and medical comorbidities
stage_groups.clinical_meaning: Requires modified treatment approach with reduced toxicity regimens; limited prospective data for alternatives to standard therapies
stage_groups.five_yr_survival: 2-year PFS approximately 89% with nivolumab-AVD in patients ≥60 years (S1826 subset analysis); 2-year EFS approximately 80% with sequential BV-AVD (Evens et al)
stage_groups.treatment_intent: Curative, with goal of minimizing toxicity while maximizing efficacy
